I couldn't find any information about the plant "Therofon jamesii". However, I can provide information on the plant "Thoroughwort" or "James' Thoroughwort" which is a plant of the genus Thoroughwort (Eupatorium spp.) but more specifically a member of the Eupatorium genus, that is similar to Eupatorium jamesii. If that is what you are looking for then here is a short paragraph:

Thoroughwort (Eupatorium jamesii) is a flowering plant in the sunflower family that is native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, making it useful for treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Thoroughwort include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Thoroughwort include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, coughs, and other respiratory ailments.
